syllabusy/AAI.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with artificial intelligence algorithms currently used in scientific research and industry, with particular emphasis on understanding and implementing artificial neural network architectures used in image analysis, tabular data analysis, and natural language processing. An additional objective of the course is to develop students’ ability to search for information on the latest achievements in artificial intelligence and open datasets useful for analyzing specific problems.
syllabusy/ADM.json: The aim of the course is to provide knowledge and develop skills in IT systems administration in a corporate environment, in particular the installation, configuration, and management of Windows Server and network services, with consideration of security and automation aspects.
syllabusy/AIC.json: Cybersecurity incident analysis is the process of investigating and assessing events that may constitute a cybersecurity breach. The objective is to understand how the incident occurred, what its consequences are, how it can be mitigated, and how similar incidents can be prevented in the future. Throughout this process, it is essential to possess the appropriate skills in system and network analysis, as well as the ability to take rapid and effective action to minimize the impact of incidents on cybersecurity.
syllabusy/ALG.json: The aim of the course is to acquire skills in using the apparatus of set theory, complex numbers, and polynomial rings; formulating problems in terms of matrices and performing matrix operations; solving systems of linear equations; and understanding basic algebraic structures. Basic issues in analytic geometry in the plane and three-dimensional space are also discussed.
syllabusy/AMM.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with modern distributed systems architectures, with particular emphasis on the microservices and microfrontends approach. Students acquire the ability to design, implement, and deploy systems composed of independent, communicating services, as well as to build scalable user interfaces composed of autonomous frontend modules. The course develops competencies required in industry, where monolithic systems are gradually being replaced by flexible cloud architectures.
syllabusy/AMs.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basic concepts of differential calculus for real-valued functions of one variable, such as sequence limits, limits of functions of one variable, continuous functions, derivatives, indefinite and definite integrals, numerical series, functional series, and differential equations. Basic applications of derivatives and integrals are presented, including approximations, extrema, and the calculation of area and volume. Connections with economics and optimization in information technologies are also indicated.
syllabusy/ANG1-3.json: The aim of the course is to teach students general English supplemented with elements of specialist language, i.e. business, graphics, new media arts, and typically academic content, at the target B2 level. Students entering the course with at least B2 proficiency achieve C1 or C2 level. The course includes specialist vocabulary, communication skills in business, especially the ability to prepare and deliver a professional presentation, as well as the development of basic communication skills, particularly rapid reading of specialist texts, especially in the field of graphics (press articles, books, websites devoted to the subject), and listening to texts of increasing difficulty. During the course, students become familiar with graphics-related terminology and prepare to communicate on professional topics at work. Abstract in English: The aim of the course is to develop students’ knowledge by expanding their general English vocabulary with elements specific to business and the field of study (graphic design), to teach communication skills, and to raise awareness of cross-cultural aspects of business communication, as well as to revise and expand general language competence to the B2–C2 level, depending on the students’ initial proficiency. Business communication skills are transferable to one’s native language. Secondary-school graduates are usually deficient in this area. These skills and abilities are indispensable in the modern work environment, regardless of sector and field of specialization.
syllabusy/ANG1.json: English language instruction at the B1/B2 level. Students acquire English proficiency that allows them to communicate in most everyday and professional situations, express thoughts and opinions, understand and produce short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn specialist vocabulary in IT and technology.
syllabusy/ANG2.json: English language instruction at the B1+/B2 level. Students further develop communication skills in English in professional and academic settings, with particular emphasis on technical vocabulary in computer science. They improve receptive skills (reading and listening) and productive skills (speaking and writing) based on specialist texts.
syllabusy/ANG3.json: English language instruction at the B2/B2+ level. Students acquire English proficiency that enables fluent professional and academic communication, technical documentation writing, delivering presentations, and participation in international IT projects. The course prepares students for a B2-level certification exam.
syllabusy/ANK.json: The aim of the course is to prepare students for more versatile work in the game development industry as creators of dynamic digital content related to various forms of animation. Emphasis is placed on the use of parametric and hierarchical mechanisms, the creation of 3D rigs, and related topics such as Disney’s 12 principles of animation, motion capture, motion simulation, simplification and editing of motion curves, creating mechanics and mechanisms with elements of engineering calculations and scripting, and managing dynamic data such as mock-ups, poses, shape keys, and control drivers/constraints.
syllabusy/ASD.json: Mastery of the analysis of iterative and recursive programs and the use of mathematical tools to analyze algorithmic complexity and knowledge of the complexity of algorithmic problems, complexity classes, and their practical significance; analysis of the complexity of sorting and searching algorithms; trees, stacks, queues, and hash tables; dynamic programming and greedy algorithms.
syllabusy/BSI.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the principles and methods of information protection in computer systems and networks.
syllabusy/BYT.json: The aim of this lecture series is to familiarize students with the basic issues of software engineering, including the phases of software development and evolution, as well as methods of improving software quality. The lecture concerns the fundamental aspects of software engineering and is organized according to the subsequent phases of the software life cycle. The following phases are discussed: strategy, requirements gathering, analysis, design, construction, testing, installation, maintenance, deployment, and support. The lecture also presents issues related to the architecture of IT systems and good design practices using CASE tools. Significant importance is attached to the discussion of current software life-cycle models and criteria for selecting a development strategy, as well as to the use of design patterns, standard APIs, and good practices in system design and implementation, particularly for distributed systems, critical systems, real-time systems, and reusable systems. Topics discussed in class also include software quality and its metrics, basic information on software testing, software project management, software configuration management, implementation, and outsourcing issues. The course also characterizes IT systems supporting business processes by discussing their most important features. The classes are correlated with the lecture and project course IT Project (PRO), ensuring a comprehensive presentation of software engineering issues and giving students the opportunity to independently and methodically carry out small IT projects—from problem definition, through strategy selection and solution planning, to designing and implementing practical product elements, using patterns and design components, and conducting system and validation tests. The reference model is the use of object technology in waterfall, component-based, or agile cycles, although other development strategies are also possible, e.g. for specific products such as computer games.
syllabusy/COV.json: Introduction to the basic problems of computer vision, methods for solving them, and evaluation of the prepared solutions.
syllabusy/CPP.json: Practical ability to program in modern C++. Particular emphasis is placed on creating efficient and clear code that makes use of the current capabilities of the language.
syllabusy/DDO.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the concept of non-relational document databases and their applications in modern IT systems. Students learn the differences between the relational and document-oriented approaches, the principles of modeling data in document format (e.g. JSON), basic data operations, and the integration of document databases with applications. The course prepares students to make informed choices about database technology depending on the nature of the problem and the system requirements.
syllabusy/DEV.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with containerization techniques using Docker and Kubernetes. The expected outcome of the course is the ability to create a REST API application packaged with the above-mentioned tools.
syllabusy/DGR.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the concept of graph databases and their applications in systems where relationships between data are of key importance. Students learn the basic elements of the graph model (vertices, edges, properties), principles of data modeling, and execution of graph queries. The course prepares students to design and implement simple graph database solutions and to compare this approach consciously with other database models.
syllabusy/DL.json: Introduction to the latest directions in the development of artificial intelligence and state-of-the-art solutions. Introduction to work on real scientific and industrial problems.
syllabusy/DOT.json: The course aims to familiarize students with the basics of the C# language and selected libraries related to .NET technologies (e.g. ASP.NET).
syllabusy/DSL.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the concept of key–value databases and their applications in modern IT systems. Students learn the characteristics of this model, the principles of storing and retrieving data, as well as typical use cases such as cache, user sessions, or application configuration. The course prepares students to make informed decisions when using key–value databases as part of IT systems architecture.
syllabusy/DTH.json: Introduction to Design Thinking methodology as a structured approach to solving problems and creating innovative IT solutions. Students learn how to build products (applications, systems, games) based on deep empathy for the user, rapid low-fidelity prototyping, and a process of continuous iteration based on received feedback.
syllabusy/ELK.json: The aim of the course is to provide students with basic knowledge in electronics, electrical engineering, and measurement techniques. Students learn the principles of operation and applications of electronic components and circuits such as resistors, capacitors, coils, diodes, transistors, and logic gates. They also learn the basics of measurement and diagnostic techniques using multimeters, oscilloscopes, and other measuring instruments.
syllabusy/FDL.json: Introduction to the latest directions in the development of artificial intelligence and state-of-the-art solutions. Introduction to work on real scientific and industrial problems.
syllabusy/FIZ.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize computer science students with the fundamentals of physics at a general academic level, in particular: (1) basic physical quantities in the field of kinematics and dynamics of a material point and a rigid body, (2) the laws of dynamics and the law of universal gravitation, (3) conservation laws in mechanics, (4) the characteristics of mechanical vibrations and wave motion, (5) the basic quantities describing electric and magnetic fields, and (6) the fundamental laws of thermodynamics.
syllabusy/GRK.json: The aim of the course is to acquire the ability to create images using standard graphics APIs (the OpenGL library and the Blender renderer) and to perform basic 2D and 3D image transformations.
syllabusy/HIS1.json: Spanish language instruction at the A1/A2 level. Students acquire Spanish proficiency enabling basic communication in everyday situations, expression of thoughts and opinions, and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Spain and Spanish-speaking countries.
syllabusy/HIS2.json: Continuation of Spanish language instruction at the A2 level. Students acquire language skills that enable communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts. The course includes vocabulary expansion, reinforcement of basic grammatical tenses, and familiarization with elements of the culture of Spain and Spanish-speaking countries.
syllabusy/HIS3.json: Spanish language instruction at the A2+ level. Students acquire language skills enabling them to describe past events, express future plans, and communicate in a professional environment. The course covers past tenses, vocabulary related to health and travel, and elements of the culture of Spanish-speaking countries.
syllabusy/HIS4.json: Spanish language instruction from A2 to B1 level. Students acquire Spanish proficiency enabling communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts, as well as expressing thoughts and opinions and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Spain and Spanish-speaking countries.
syllabusy/HKJ.json: Introduction to the history and culture of Japan, including its most important achievements in the field of art and aesthetics. Familiarization with traditions, customs, norms of social behavior, problems of contemporary Japanese society, as well as modern and historical architecture.
syllabusy/ICK.json: The aim of the course is to develop students’ knowledge and skills in the field of human–computer interaction (HCI) design by introducing the theory and methodology of creating useful, accessible, and efficient user interfaces, with particular emphasis on user needs analysis, user journey design, information architecture, prototyping, and usability evaluation in accordance with the principles of the User-Centred Design (UCD) approach. The course has a theoretical and practical character and includes the analysis of existing interactive systems as well as the design and testing of students’ own solutions based on accepted standards and good practices.
syllabusy/IML.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basic issues of machine learning (including elements of deep learning), mainly using neural networks (including convolutional neural networks), as well as with methods for programming selected techniques and the practical use of ready-made tools.
syllabusy/JAP1.json: Japanese language instruction from scratch. Students acquire Japanese proficiency enabling communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts, as well as expressing thoughts and opinions and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Japan and Japanese-speaking regions.
syllabusy/JAP2.json: Japanese language instruction from scratch. Students acquire Japanese proficiency enabling communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts, as well as expressing thoughts and opinions and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Japan and Japanese-speaking regions.
syllabusy/JAP3.json: Japanese language instruction from scratch. Students acquire Japanese proficiency enabling communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts, as well as expressing thoughts and opinions and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Japan and Japanese-speaking regions.
syllabusy/JAP4.json: Japanese language instruction from scratch. Students acquire Japanese proficiency enabling communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts, as well as expressing thoughts and opinions and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Japan and Japanese-speaking regions.
syllabusy/JFS.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of functional programming in F# and to develop the ability to create simple applications running on the .NET platform. The course places emphasis on data immutability, higher-order functions, pattern matching, and safe data modeling. It prepares students to work with modern programming paradigms and to make informed tool choices within the .NET environment.
syllabusy/JJA.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of programming in Java and to develop the ability to create simple applications using the object-oriented paradigm. The course places emphasis on proper class modeling, code readability, exception handling, and the use of standard Java libraries. It prepares students for further study of JVM-based technologies and for the implementation of small programming projects.
syllabusy/JPC.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of programming in C++ and to develop the ability to create simple console applications using classical programming constructs. The course places emphasis on understanding the memory model, correct use of data types, functions and structures, as well as an introduction to the basics of object-oriented programming. It prepares students for further study of compiled languages and for work on projects requiring greater control over performance and resources.
syllabusy/JPT.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of programming in Python and to develop the ability to independently create simple applications and scripts. The course places emphasis on code readability, good programming practices, and practical applications of Python in data analysis, task automation, and building simple applications. It prepares students for further study of technologies that use Python and for the implementation of small programming projects.
syllabusy/JSC.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of programming in Scala, combining the object-oriented and functional paradigms, and to develop the ability to create simple applications running on the JVM platform. The course places emphasis on data immutability, functional expressions, pattern matching, and clear, safe code. It prepares students for further study of JVM-based technologies and for work with modern functional languages.
syllabusy/KCs.json: Digital forensics is a field concerned with the investigation and examination of evidence related to digital crimes. It is a branch of forensic science focused on the ability to use technology and scientific methods to gather evidence that can be presented in court. Digital forensics plays a key role in combating cybercrime by helping law enforcement understand how a crime occurred, who may be responsible, and what steps can be taken to prevent future incidents. The main educational objectives include network investigation, i.e. the ability to analyze network traffic in order to detect irregularities or evidence of criminal activity. Another objective is system log analysis, i.e. collecting information about what occurred on a given system at a specific time. Analyzing such logs can help determine what happened during an incident.
syllabusy/KIP.json: The educational objective of the course 'Communication and Protocols for the Internet of Things' is to understand how IoT devices exchange data across different communication layers and how to select a protocol depending on application requirements such as range, energy consumption, throughput, reliability, and security. Students learn how to design device-to-cloud and device-to-device communication, prepare data formats, and ensure system interoperability. They also develop practical skills in configuring and testing transmission, diagnosing network problems, and assessing link quality and latency.
syllabusy/KLI.json: Preparing students to take on leadership roles in technical teams (Tech Lead, Team Leader, Scrum Master). The course places emphasis on the development of emotional intelligence, the ability to build effective teams, and process management in a dynamic IT environment. Students learn communication techniques, conflict resolution, and methodologies supporting team autonomy.
syllabusy/KMR.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the process of building a start-up venture from scratch. Students are introduced to business models and the commercialization of innovative solutions, and then begin applying the acquired knowledge in practice on the basis of an engineering project.
syllabusy/KNO.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with various forms of knowledge representation and inference systems that can be used by IT systems for problem solving.
syllabusy/KPIR.json: The main objective of the course is to present the structure and operation of Internet of Things systems, in particular the methods and means of communication, including protocols that enable information exchange between sensors, actuators, and controllers. A characteristic feature of IoT systems is the use of low-power devices and operation in conditions requiring secure transmission and data processing.
syllabusy/KSI.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with techniques for containerizing web applications and orchestrating containers in production environments. Students learn the Docker ecosystem—from building images, through managing networks and volumes, to automating multi-container environments with Docker Compose. In the later part of the course, students are introduced to Kubernetes as the standard for cloud container orchestration, learning how to deploy, scale, and monitor applications. The course prepares students for work in modern DevOps and cloud environments (AWS, GCP, Azure).
syllabusy/LANG1.json: English language instruction at the B2+ level. Students improve their language skills in professional and academic contexts related to computer science. The course places emphasis on productive skills (writing documentation, giving presentations) and communication in a project environment.
syllabusy/LANG2.json: English language instruction at the B2+/C1 level. Students further develop professional communication skills, technical writing, specialist presentations, and participation in international IT projects. The course prepares students for a C1-level certification exam.
syllabusy/LANG3.json: English language instruction at the C1 level. Students achieve professional and academic fluency in English enabling them to independently conduct projects, write diploma theses, and participate in scientific conferences.
syllabusy/LANG4.json: English language instruction at the C1/C2 level. Final semester of the language course—students refine the language skills necessary to defend their diploma thesis and enter the international IT job market.
syllabusy/LLP.json: The educational objective of the course is to understand how a computer executes a program at the level of processor architecture and instructions, and to acquire practical skills in x86 assembly programming. Students learn to work consciously with memory, the stack, control flow (jumps, loops), and arithmetic, as well as to design and organize code.
syllabusy/M3D.json: The aim of the course is to prepare students for work in the game development industry as creators of digital content. Emphasis is placed on 3D modeling and related topics such as texturing, material creation, baking, optimization techniques, and strategies related to game development.
syllabusy/MAD.json: Introduction to the basic mathematical apparatus and fundamental concepts of discrete mathematics. Propositional calculus, set theory, combinatorics, probability theory, number theory, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs.
syllabusy/MAS.json: The main objective of the course is to establish students’ understanding of the importance of modeling in systems engineering and to equip them with techniques and tools for modeling systems using formalisms such as UML, BPMN, and Petri nets. As tools for analyzing system properties, students are also introduced to the concepts of S-invariants and T-invariants in Petri nets, as well as basic notions of temporal logics LTL and CTL.
syllabusy/MFL.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of developing cross-platform mobile applications using the Flutter framework and the Dart language. Students learn the architecture of widget-based applications, the principles of user interface design, state management, and integration with external services. The classes are practical in nature and prepare students to independently create simple applications for Android and iOS.
syllabusy/MHE.json: The course covers metaheuristic methods. It provides an overview of techniques in this field, together with methods for testing and comparing different approaches to optimization problems. Students become familiar with the properties and applications of heuristic algorithms. The theoretical knowledge is reinforced in laboratory classes, which provide a more practical understanding of the subject and prepare students to decide whether metaheuristic methods or other techniques are more appropriate for a given task.
syllabusy/MLR.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with selected issues in machine learning (including elements of deep learning), mainly using neural networks (including convolutional neural networks), as well as methods for programming selected techniques and the practical use of ready-made tools and libraries.
syllabusy/MNE.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of developing cross-platform mobile and desktop applications using the .NET MAUI platform. Students learn the architecture of applications based on the MVVM pattern, user interface development in XAML, state management, and integration with native features of Android, iOS, and Windows systems. The classes are practical in nature and prepare students to independently create simple cross-platform applications.
syllabusy/MRN.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of mobile application development using the React Native framework. Students learn the architecture of component-based applications, state management, navigation, and integration with native mobile device features. The classes are practical in nature and prepare students to independently create simple cross-platform mobile applications for Android and iOS.
syllabusy/MUN.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basics of creating games and interactive 3D applications using the Unity engine and the C# language. Students learn the architecture of the engine, working with scene objects, components, the physics system, and the basics of user interaction. The classes are practical in nature and prepare students to independently complete simple 3D projects, also with mobile platforms in mind.
syllabusy/NAI.json: The goal of the course is to familiarize students with the basic techniques used in artificial intelligence. These techniques are increasingly becoming part of mainstream IT technologies and are playing a growing role in industrial applications. Moreover, a large portion of the material discussed has a broader scope and can help future engineers solve IT problems beyond the strict domain of AI.
syllabusy/NIM1.json: German language instruction from A1 to A2 level. Students acquire German proficiency enabling communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts, as well as expressing thoughts and opinions and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Germany and other German-speaking countries.
syllabusy/NIM2.json: German language instruction at the B1/B1+ level. Students acquire German proficiency enabling communication in most everyday and professional situations, expressing thoughts and opinions, and understanding and producing written texts and spoken statements on general topics and specialist topics related to computer science.
syllabusy/NIM3.json: German language instruction from A2 to B1 level. Students acquire German proficiency enabling communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts, as well as expressing thoughts and opinions and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Germany and other German-speaking countries.
syllabusy/NIM4.json: German language instruction from A2 to B1 level. Students acquire German proficiency enabling communication in most everyday situations and, to some extent, professional contexts, as well as expressing thoughts and opinions and understanding and producing short written texts and spoken statements on various topics. Students also learn about the culture, history, and everyday life of Germany and other German-speaking countries.
syllabusy/OGL.json: The aim of the course is to acquire the ability to use the advanced capabilities of the OpenGL library.
syllabusy/PAI.json: The aim of the course is to acquire practical skills in designing and implementing modern web applications divided into backend and frontend layers, based on Domain-Driven Design (DDD) and hexagonal architecture. Students learn the basics of building SOA-style solutions, authentication and authorization mechanisms (OAuth 2.1), containerization, and software engineering practices including integration and end-to-end testing. The intended outcome is the ability to deliver a working application together with automated setup and testing.
syllabusy/PBA.json:
This course aims to provide students with comprehensive knowledge and skills necessary for designing and maintaining secure IT systems. By combining theoretical lectures with practical exercises, students are better prepared to face cybersecurity challenges in real-world environments.

Students learn the basic concepts and principles of information security, such as confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data. They learn how to design IT systems and infrastructure resistant to a variety of threats and attacks. The course also aims to develop an understanding of identity management and access control mechanisms, including the implementation of security policies.

The course covers secure programming techniques and best practices that help prevent vulnerabilities from being introduced already at the software development stage. Students also learn how to respond to security incidents, including conducting incident analysis and preparing breach reports.
syllabusy/PG1.json: Understanding the prototype life cycle from concept to a functional digital prototype. Learning to identify design flaws early and eliminate unplayable mechanics with minimal effort. Practical use of game engines, ready-made tools, and scripts for rapid implementation of game logic without the need to build full production systems. Ability to evaluate gameplay, develop critical analysis of the feedback loop and player experience (game feel) at an early production stage. Visual and technical communication, and preparation for presenting ideas in the form of a Vertical Slice or a Minimum Viable Product (MVP).
syllabusy/PG2.json: Development of skills in comprehensive game design as multimedia products. Emphasis is placed on integrating advanced design techniques with visual (Art), usability (UX/UI), and business (Marketing/Market) aspects. Carrying a project from an advanced prototype to the vertical slice stage while ensuring aesthetic coherence and effective communication with the audience.
syllabusy/PJN.json: Introduction to the basic problems of computer vision, methods for solving them, and evaluation of the developed solutions.
syllabusy/POJ.json: The aim of the classes is to familiarize students with the basic concepts of object-oriented programming using C# and the .NET platform as examples. The following notions are discussed: interface, class, object, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation, and composition. Concepts and practical aspects related to the use of generic collections, exceptions, delegates and events, input/output operations, and selected elements of modern C# (including LINQ) are presented.
syllabusy/POZ.json: The aim of the course is to discuss fundamental economic issues: the economy, the economic and social system; money and the market; technological, ethical, and legal determinants; the information- and knowledge-based economy; marketing; the basics of accounting; forms of business activity; and establishing and managing a company.
syllabusy/PPS.json: The course aims to develop skills in designing, programming, and optimizing a variety of hardware platforms, which is invaluable in today’s technological reality. It introduces typical platforms on which IoT projects can be started. These platforms are also used in finished products.
syllabusy/PRG1.json: The aim of the classes is to help students master the basics of classical structured programming techniques using C# as the foundation. Elements of object-oriented programming are also introduced. The classes are intended to develop abstract thinking and the ability to solve simple programming problems using the .NET platform and console applications.
syllabusy/PRI.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with basic analytical and design techniques, CASE tools, and the methodological foundations of developing IT solutions. Students are expected to demonstrate knowledge of the most commonly used pre-project documents, core modeling categories and UML diagrams, as well as methods of mapping the object model into a selected programming language or relational schema using tool-supported transformation techniques.
syllabusy/PRIN.json: The course 'Innovation Processes' aims to familiarize students with the basics of managing an innovative company and the organizational foundations of its operation, as well as with techniques for analyzing and stimulating creativity and for creatively solving engineering problems.
syllabusy/PRO.json: The aim of the classes is to enhance skills related to the fundamental issues and practice of planning and managing an IT project, in particular a project aimed at producing a software product using object-oriented technology, and to provide students with experience in teamwork. The project classes are coordinated with the lecture course Systems Construction and Integration (BYT), ensuring a comprehensive treatment of software engineering issues and giving students the opportunity to independently and methodically carry out small IT projects—from problem definition, through strategy selection and planning of the solution, to implementation of practical product elements using patterns and design components, as well as conducting system and validation tests and documenting them. The reference project model is the use of object technology in a waterfall or component-based cycle, as well as agile project implementation strategies. Important factors in topic selection include practical usefulness and, where possible, cooperation with a real project client. Topics are carried out in groups of 2–4 students.
syllabusy/PRZ1.json: As part of the course, students choose and carry out, under the supervision of a thesis advisor, a team engineering project related thematically to their chosen specialization. Their solution to a practical problem should employ an appropriate implementation approach and modern, well-selected technologies, and in addition to various technical dimensions should also take into account economic, ergonomic, legal, and social aspects. The documented solution developed within the project is continued in Team Project 2 and, together with a description of the individual contribution, constitutes the content of the engineering diploma thesis; it may also serve as a 'pre-incubation' product.
syllabusy/PRZ2.json: As part of the course, students continue the project started in Team Project PRZ1, thematically related to their specialization. The documented solution developed within the course constitutes part of the engineering diploma thesis. The solution proposed by the team to a practical problem should be innovative and as comprehensive as possible, taking into account, in addition to various technical dimensions, also economic, ergonomic, legal, and social aspects; it may also serve as a 'pre-incubation' product. Typically, depending on the adopted strategy, PRZ2 involves evolutionary increments (subsequent releases) of projects based on the release created in PRZ1.
syllabusy/PSEM.json: The aim of the classes is to prepare students for developing and presenting diploma theses, to discipline them in the process of team-based completion of the work, and to prepare them for the diploma examination. The classes are conducted and discussed in specialization groups and include preparation, seminar presentation, and discussion of solutions and decisions adopted during the completion of diploma theses. An important educational element is the analysis and discussion of design solutions, the roles and tasks of project stakeholders, and the social or business context of the work.
syllabusy/PTT.json: The educational objective of the course 'Rapid Prototyping' is to prepare students in the IoT specialization to move efficiently from an idea to a working device prototype. Students learn to design and manufacture mechanical elements using 3D printing and to integrate them with electronics (prototype boards, wiring, connectors) while observing the principles of reliability and work safety. They develop practical skills in assembling and starting up systems: soldering, component selection, testing, and diagnosing typical prototype errors.
syllabusy/PUI.json: Students of engineering programs, including computer science, often fail to recognize the need to develop their non-technical skills, referred to in the literature as so-called soft skills. Moreover, the significance and importance of, for example, communication skills or teamwork are often downplayed or underestimated by future engineers. Meanwhile, employers’ expectations in the IT industry have changed significantly compared with what was expected just a few years ago. The ability to effectively organize one’s own work, maintain focus, and solve problems, also in cooperation with others, is no longer merely an advantage, but in some cases a deciding factor in being accepted into a company or project. The course is an original proposal addressed to students studying computer science. Within the course, awareness and habits related to developing personal resources concerning motivation, intellectual potential, and functioning in a team are shaped. Students gain knowledge and skills regarding key non-technical competencies necessary for the effective completion of team IT projects, as well as for building a competitive advantage on the labor market. The adopted approach takes into account the specific profile of engineering education, giving students a foundation for applying the acquired skills in practice. As part of the independent work module, students become familiar with many techniques and tools that optimize the completion of tasks often performed under time pressure and demanding expectations, requiring concentration, error detection, or the search for new ideas and solutions. In addition, they will be able to plan their own development in the IT industry more effectively, understanding the need for continuous acquisition of skills and further education. As part of the teamwork module, students identify key challenges and problems they will face during project implementation. They also learn strategies that improve efficiency in working with others—in the context of communication, task division and estimation, and responsibility. An important element of the course is the guest participation of Faculty graduates and representatives of IT companies, who share their experience in line with the topic of a given lecture.
syllabusy/RBD.json: Students are able to prepare a database schema in SQL, ready for implementation in any database management system, meeting client expectations, correct from the point of view of normalization, and equipped with technical documentation (including an ERD diagram and explanations of any triggers or procedures used).
syllabusy/RTO.json: The educational objective of the course 'Real-Time Systems' is to understand the principles of designing deterministic software and to gain practical mastery of working with FreeRTOS in embedded IoT systems. Students learn how to model tasks, choose priorities and scheduling parameters, and analyze execution times and sources of delays affecting the ability to meet deadlines. During the course, they develop skills in safe communication and synchronization between tasks (queues, semaphores, mutexes, event groups) and in diagnosing problems typical of RTOS environments (race conditions, deadlocks, priority inversion).
syllabusy/S3D.json: The subject matter of the course is based on simulation engines available in open-source software (Blender). Topics include simulations of systems with inertia and friction in a rigid-body model, simulations of soft bodies with deformable surfaces, cloth and fabric simulations, particle simulations in Newtonian, Fluid, Boids, and Keyed models, as well as simulations of fire, smoke, explosions, sparks, dust, fog, and other volumetric phenomena. In addition, topics from the field of Dynamic Paint are discussed, where the simulation system affects the simulated system, for example simulating waves on water, destruction, deformations, reflections, changes in medium density, etc. The course extends and enriches the knowledge acquired in the Computer Graphics course.
syllabusy/SAD.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with methods of data presentation and analysis as well as probabilistic modeling tools; to present methods of mathematical statistics and statistical inference; and to equip students with practical skills in using software for statistical data analysis.
syllabusy/SAI.json: Introduction to the social aspects of the development of digital technologies, their use and misuse, taking into account the professional and ethical responsibility of computer scientists, legal issues, and the impact of technology on individuals, professional groups, and society. Issues of interpersonal communication and conflict resolution are also discussed.
syllabusy/SCR.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basic issues related to real-time systems (SCR), including the principles of operation and the specific nature of real-time systems and real-time operating systems, as well as the methodology of developing real-time applications. This enables students to acquire practical skills in creating real-time applications for a specified system specification and selected hardware.
syllabusy/SGD.json: The course 'Simulations and Decision Games' aims to familiarize students with selected decision-support methods, develop the ability to build decision models using software, and develop the ability to make decisions in dynamic decision-making environments. In its practical component, the course also allows students to learn how to create games with an emphasis on designing logic and strategy.
syllabusy/SGK.json: The aim of the course is to deepen students’ knowledge of contemporary game engines using Unity 6 as the main example, with comparisons to Unreal Engine and Godot in the context of selecting technology appropriate to the scale and type of project. Students learn advanced Unity engine systems such as render pipelines (URP/HDRP/Built-In) and the differences between them, the ECS/DOTS architecture and its impact on performance, asset management methods such as Asset Bundles, Addressables, and Streaming Assets, the New Input System, and the Unity Localization Package. Students also learn good practices and architectural approaches that support Unity integration with external platforms.
syllabusy/SITA.json: This course aims to provide students with comprehensive knowledge and skills necessary for designing and maintaining secure IT systems. By combining theoretical lectures with practical exercises, students are better prepared to face cybersecurity challenges in real-world environments. Students learn the basic concepts and principles of information security, such as confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data. They learn how to design IT systems and infrastructure resistant to a variety of threats and attacks. The course also aims to develop an understanding of identity management and access control mechanisms, including the implementation of security policies. The course covers secure programming techniques and best practices that help prevent vulnerabilities from being introduced already at the software development stage. Students also learn how to respond to security incidents, including conducting incident analysis and reporting breaches.
syllabusy/SKOA.json: The course is devoted to presenting the principles of operation of contemporary ICT networks based on the OSI layered model. The functions of individual OSI layers and the relationships between them, as well as the technologies, standards, and protocols used, are discussed. Students acquire the skills necessary to prepare a LAN network design, including IPv4 and IPv6 configuration, router and switch encapsulation, routing, and basic protection of devices against attacks from inside and outside networks.
syllabusy/SOP.json: The educational objective of the course SOP (Operating Systems and Programming) is to familiarize students with advanced operating system mechanisms, particularly in the context of process management, memory management, and synchronization in multithreaded and multiprocessor environments. The course places emphasis on practical aspects of working with the Linux system, including resource management, threads, interrupts, semaphores, and mutexes.
syllabusy/SPR.json: Introduction to the basic techniques of rapid prototyping.
syllabusy/SWB.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the structure and basic methods of designing and programming embedded systems, mainly those based on microcontrollers, i.e. specialized IT systems responsible for performing strictly defined tasks, usually related to monitoring and control. The physical principles of operation of sensors (receptors) and actuators, as the basic components of such systems, are also discussed.
syllabusy/TAPI.json: The course 'Trends in API Development' aims to familiarize students with current directions of development and innovations in the field of creating APIs for web applications. Students acquire theoretical and practical knowledge of the latest standards, protocols, and tools used for designing, implementing, and managing APIs. Participants have the opportunity to explore issues such as API security, scalability, performance, and good programming practices. They also become familiar with different API styles, including REST, GraphQL, gRPC, and others, analyzing their advantages, disadvantages, and applications in various project contexts. Another important element of the course is group work, which aims to develop collaboration, communication, and innovative thinking skills. Students are tasked with analyzing and preparing material on a selected API-related technology, allowing them to deepen their knowledge and practical skills in a specific area.
syllabusy/TBK.json: The course 'Backend Technologies' was created to develop skills in advanced programming in JavaScript using Node.js and an example server-side environment-building tool based on the HTTP protocol, with the goal of creating efficient and scalable web applications. The curriculum focuses on a deep understanding of the HTTP protocol and the REST API pattern, which enables effective design and implementation of APIs responsible for communication between the frontend and backend.
syllabusy/TBS.json: The aim of the course is not only to teach the theoretical foundations of securing IT systems, but also to provide practical skills in identifying and fixing security vulnerabilities. As a result, students are better prepared to secure IT systems in real-world scenarios.
syllabusy/TFN.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with modern technologies, techniques, and tools for developing graphical user interfaces in web applications.
syllabusy/TIN.json: Introduction to modern web technologies that are essential for creating and managing dynamic websites and web applications. The course provides knowledge on building responsive interfaces, using advanced CSS techniques, and programming in JavaScript, including both functional and object-oriented approaches. It also covers asynchronous programming, DOM manipulation, and the use of networking tools such as HTTP and WebSocket. In addition, students learn the basics of working with a popular tool for building server-side applications.
syllabusy/UKOS.json: The educational objectives of the course include familiarizing students with the basics of working in Linux, including command-line operation and task automation using bash scripts. It is also essential to understand how version control systems (e.g. Git) work, which supports effective teamwork in programming projects. Secondary objectives include more practical skills such as creating functions and scripts to automate everyday programming tasks, working with files, packaging data, and using tools such as Midnight Commander and FTP.
syllabusy/WDZ.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the basic concepts and principles of organizational management. Students learn the functions of management (planning, organizing, leading, controlling), organizational structures, leadership styles, and elements of project and team management. The course serves as an introduction to understanding the business environment in which IT projects operate and develops managerial awareness necessary in the work of an IT engineer.
syllabusy/WF1.json: Developing the ability to recognize and assess one’s own physical fitness and physical development, encouraging participation in sports and recreational forms of physical activity, learning and applying safety rules during physical activity, understanding the relationship between physical activity and health, and developing the ability to practice health-promoting behaviors. Classes take place in the swimming pool, sauna, gym, and sports hall.
syllabusy/WF2.json: Developing the ability to recognize and assess one’s own physical fitness and physical development, encouraging participation in sports and recreational forms of physical activity, learning and applying safety rules during physical activity, understanding the relationship between physical activity and health, and developing the ability to practice health-promoting behaviors. Classes take place in the swimming pool, sauna, gym, and sports hall.
syllabusy/WG1.json: The aim of this course is to immerse students in the game creation cycle, from conceptual design to quality control, and to instill a deep understanding of industry practices, tools (such as Unreal Engine), and key processes necessary for a successful career in the games industry.
syllabusy/WG2.json: The aim of the course is to deepen students’ knowledge and skills in game development, focusing on advanced game design techniques, optimization, marketing, and market analysis. Students gain practical experience in creating games that run smoothly on different platforms and learn how to effectively promote their products on the gaming market.
syllabusy/WPR.json: The aim of the course is to develop in students practical workshop skills of a programmer, in particular in the use of programming tools, code debugging, teamwork using the Git version control system and the GitHub platform. The classes aim to familiarize students with the basics of process automation (CI/CD), debugging web applications using browser developer tools, the fundamentals of web technologies (HTML, CSS, JavaScript), as well as introducing the conscious and responsible use of AI-based tools in a programmer’s work. An additional objective is to prepare students to build their own programming portfolio.
syllabusy/ZIC.json: The aim of the course is to familiarize students with the Infrastructure as Code (IaC) methodology and the tools and platforms enabling the automatic creation, configuration, and management of cloud infrastructure at production scale. Students acquire practical skills in working with Terraform (resource provisioning), Ansible (configuration management and automation), Amazon Web Services (public cloud services), and OpenStack together with its local emulator LocalStack (private cloud and testing environments). The course develops the competencies of a DevOps/Cloud engineer who can design repeatable, versioned, and auditable infrastructure environments.
syllabusy/ZPR.json: Introduction to the key concepts and practices of IT project management. Equipping students with the skills to plan, organize, lead, and control IT projects. Understanding the specifics of managing IT teams, motivation, and communication.
syllabusy/ZZGA.json: This course is intended for advanced students and, as such, constitutes a unique offering among Polish universities, as very few institutions provide classes at such an advanced level. The subject of the course is the creation of effects understood as 'practical effects' (invisible effects) and 'special effects' (visible effects). The techniques presented are used in studios in Los Angeles during work on major productions. Practical effects involve modifying an image in such a way that the modifications are not visible. The possibilities here are very broad, and each topic is unique. During this course, students are expected to work on effects such as adding birds in the background, inserting smoke and fire, removing unwanted elements (signs, text, etc.), creating a crowd from a handful of people, animating explosions, cracks, changes in appearance, and more. During the classes, we become familiar with techniques such as keying (greenscreen), rotoscoping (animated masks), object tracking (3D object tracking), camera tracking (photogrammetric calculation of camera movement based on frame movement), planar tracking (useful for modifying flat surfaces in recorded video), as well as various scene reconstruction techniques. If time allows, we also look at the possibilities of film editing and color grading, as well as other techniques such as matte painting or motion capture (including the possible use of free motion libraries from Carnegie Mellon University).
